gates_re_evaluated.rationale: state/coordinator/build-complete.json exists. Handoff signal correctly written.

TD's sections file should be checked for machine_checkable_assertions[] on each inflection_resolution. If absent, fail; if present, pass. Likely present given run-report sophistication, but unverified by this audit pass.
Each section's acceptance phrases should derive at least one acceptance_assertion. Likely present given the depth of the verification report; unverified.

Verification report exists but does not document an artifact-exercise pass under cv_artifact_exercise. Static inspection fields ('source grep across...') dominate. May or may not have done DOM-loaded exercise; absence of a cv-runner script (unlike kanban-board which has one) suggests static-only.

If the run did do artifact-exercise (uncertain), it would have been pre-v1.5 and likely used jsdom with stubbed runtime deps. The trainer uses ES modules and likely has no CDN deps (unlike latex), so the substitution risk is lower — but the verification still didn't run under explicit production fidelity per v1.5 wording.
No PNV in the verification report. The prompt's named verb 'train me on blackjack' (or whatever the literal verb was) was not asserted end-to-end.
history/log.jsonl exists; build-summary.md and decision-index.json existence is unverified by this audit pass.
Absent — the v1.6 schema field didn't exist when this run was built.

incidental_observations: This run was more sophisticated than the original blackjack run — has build-complete.json, full verification report with assumption checks, populated final/. It's closer to passing v1.6 than blackjack is.
The two definite failures are PNV and prompt_verb_analysis, both v1.5/v1.6 gates that postdate this run. The 'needs_inspection' items might pass; a more thorough audit pass would resolve them.
If the trainer artifact actually trains the user on blackjack correctly (which the run-report claims it does), the failures here are documentation/structure, not behavior.

recovery_options.description: Add prompt_verb_analysis to TD's sections file, derive PNV ('open page, deal a hand, observe strategy table highlights the basic-strategy correct action'), run CV artifact-exercise under v1.5 production fidelity (file:// load, real KaTeX-equivalent if any deps), produce missing Historian artifacts. Estimated effort: 2-3 dispatches.
Discard and re-run. Cleaner if the patches above touch many files.

recommendation_rationale: This run is borderline. Two definite gate failures (PNV, prompt_verb_analysis) are documentation-side and easy to add. Four 'needs_inspection' gates may pass on closer review. If a full inspection pass confirms the four are passing, only the two definites need patching, which is cheap. If multiple of the four also fail, rebuild becomes preferable. Recommend: do a 30-minute thorough inspection of the four uncertain gates first, then choose patch vs rebuild based on the count.
